gene_description: Alcohol metabolism, nitroglycerin bioactivation (Asian flush gene)

source.title: Mitochondrial ALDH2 Glu504Lys polymorphism contributes to the variation in efficacy of sublingual nitroglycerin
source.finding: ALDH2*2 (rs671 A allele) virtually eliminates nitroglycerin bioactivation to NO. 30-50% of East Asians carry this variant. Carriers show significantly reduced GTN efficacy for angina.

AA.effect: ~96% loss of ALDH2 enzyme activity. Nitroglycerin cannot be bioactivated to NO — drug is essentially ineffective. Severe alcohol flush reaction. Accumulated acetaldehyde increases esophageal cancer risk with alcohol use.
AA.recommendation: Nitroglycerin will NOT work for angina. Use alternative vasodilators (calcium channel blockers, direct NO donors). AVOID alcohol due to carcinogenic acetaldehyde accumulation.

AG.effect: ~60-80% reduced ALDH2 activity. Nitroglycerin efficacy reduced by ~33-40%. Mild-to-moderate alcohol flush. One defective subunit is sufficient to impair the ALDH2 tetramer.
AG.recommendation: Nitroglycerin may have reduced effectiveness. Consider higher dose or alternative anti-anginal. Moderate alcohol with caution.

GG.effect: Normal ALDH2 enzyme activity. Standard nitroglycerin bioactivation and efficacy. Normal alcohol metabolism.
GG.recommendation: Standard nitroglycerin dosing for angina. No ALDH2-related drug concerns.
